_comment: Entries the schema-drift guard should ignore. Each: {file (endsWith match), ref? (table.col; omit to match the whole file), reason}. Prefer FIXING drift over allowlisting; use this only for guard false-positives and explicitly-deferred work.

allow.reason: FALSE POSITIVE: the .eq('snapshot_id') applies to the gpl_snapshot_metrics query (built in a `query` variable), not gpl_snapshots. The guard's linear .from()-tracking mis-attributes filters on variable-built queries.
FALSE POSITIVE: the upsert targets `tasks` (built in a variable); extraction_item_idx is a real tasks column. Same variable-built-query attribution limitation.

DEFERRED (legacy TM module): raw SQL queries the current `tasks` table using the pre-role-simplification schema (t.assignee_id, u.full_name, status 'delayed') which no longer exists. Fixing requires migrating/removing the whole legacy task-management module (owner_user_id, name, real status set), a refactor out of scope for a drift-repair run. Pre-existing breakage; see final report.
DEFERRED (legacy TM module): same superseded-schema raw SQL as tm/cron/reminders.
DEFERRED (legacy TM module): superseded-schema raw SQL + dead role values ('dg','agency_admin').

DEFERRED (legacy TM module): every query assumes the pre-simplification tasks schema (t.assignee_id, t.created_by, users.full_name). The module (app/api/tm/**) needs a migration or removal decision, not a column rename. Pre-existing breakage; see final report.
